facts.src: California has reached 16,942 megawatts (MW) of battery storage available
13,880 MW come from large utility-scale projects
behind-the-meter battery systems installed on more than 200,000 homes (2,213 MW)
businesses, schools and local government facilities (849 MW)
an increase of about 1,200 MW in the past six months
a 2,100% surge in storage capacity since Gov. Newsom took office in 2019
the equivalent of roughly one-quarter of California’s record peak demand for several hours
At the UN Climate Change Conference in Brazil, Governor Gavin Newsom announced
The California Independent System Operator — which serves roughly 80 percent of the state’s electricity consumers
the Governor recently signed Senate Bill 283
followed by Texas with roughly 9,000 MW
(page gives only MW power figures; no MWh energy-capacity figure appears anywhere)
